define i8 @simple_recurrence_intrinsic_multiuse_phi(i8 %n, i8 %a, i8 %b) {
; CHECK-LABEL: define i8 @simple_recurrence_intrinsic_multiuse_phi(
; CHECK-SAME: i8 [[N:%.*]], i8 [[A:%.*]], i8 [[B:%.*]]) {
; CHECK-NEXT:  [[ENTRY:.*]]:
; CHECK-NEXT:    br label %[[LOOP:.*]]
; CHECK:       [[LOOP]]:
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[IV:%.*]] = phi i8 [ [[IV_NEXT:%.*]], %[[LOOP]] ], [ 0, %[[ENTRY]] ]
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[UMAX_ACC:%.*]] = phi i8 [ [[UMAX:%.*]], %[[LOOP]] ], [ [[A]], %[[ENTRY]] ]
; CHECK-NEXT:    call void @use(i8 [[UMAX_ACC]])
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[UMAX]] = call i8 @llvm.umax.i8(i8 [[A]], i8 [[B]])
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[IV_NEXT]] = add nuw i8 [[IV]], 1
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[CMP:%.*]] = icmp ult i8 [[IV_NEXT]], [[N]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    br i1 [[CMP]], label %[[LOOP]], label %[[EXIT:.*]]
; CHECK:       [[EXIT]]:
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i8 [[UMAX]]
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i8 [ %iv.next, %loop ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %umax.acc = phi i8 [ %umax, %loop ], [ %a, %entry ]
  call void @use(i8 %umax.acc)
  %umax = call i8 @llvm.umax.i8(i8 %umax.acc, i8 %b)
  %iv.next = add nuw i8 %iv, 1
  %cmp = icmp ult i8 %iv.next, %n
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret i8 %umax
}

issue.title: [SCEV] Repeated max not hoisted out of loop
issue.author: aengelke

comments.author: nikic
comments.body:
```llvm
define i8 @test(i8 %n, i8 %a, i8 %b) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:
  %iv = phi i8 [ %iv.next, %loop ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %umax.acc = phi i8 [ %umax, %loop ], [ %a, %entry ]
  %umax = call i8 @llvm.umax.i8(i8 %umax.acc, i8 %b)
  %iv.next = add nuw i8 %iv, 1
  %cmp = icmp ult i8 %iv.next, %n
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop, label %exit

exit:
  ret i8 %umax
}
```

I don't think SCEV is the right place for this, it doesn't generally reason about non-add recurrences. Possibly this should just be in InstCombine.

comments.author: dtcxzyw
comments.body: @antoniofrighetto Can you add a `matchSimpleRecurrence`-like helper for binary intrinsics in ValueTracking? I plan to extend https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/143020 to handle other reductions. Thank you!

comments.author: antoniofrighetto
comments.body: @dtcxzyw Assigning this to you too, for the remaining work to be done :)

comments.author: dtcxzyw
comments.body: @antoniofrighetto Ping. I didn't mean to take this issue. I just mean that the helper function can be reused by another patch (See https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/148616).
